header.topic: Selection: IF/ELSE branching across five notations

learning_sequence_context.position_in_sequence: Lesson 3 of 6 — Selection (foundational module, compulsory full traversal)
learning_sequence_context.previous_lesson_summary: Last lesson pupils traced pseudocode IF/ELSE examples and identified the decision diamond in a given flowchart.
learning_sequence_context.next_lesson_link: Next lesson pupils translate their flowchart solutions into BASIC-256, beginning first contact with a real parser.

expected_outcomes.lesson_objective: Pupils can construct a flowchart representation of an IF/ELSE selection structure that is behaviourally equivalent to a given pseudocode algorithm.
assessment_criteria: Each student will correctly place a decision diamond with a single boolean condition.
Each student will correctly route the True and False branches to matching pseudocode outcomes.
Each student will identify at least one discrepancy when their flowchart is traced against test data.
expected_outcomes.activity_summary: Paired flowchart construction from a given pseudocode grading algorithm, followed by peer trace-testing against provided inputs.

dok_level.rationale: Continued_learning and review_of_learning segments require pupils to construct and trace a flowchart against test data (Skills and Concepts), not merely recall the symbol set (which is DOK 1, covered in the introduction segment only).

assessment_objectives.rationale: Recall and correct use of standard flowchart symbols (introduction segment).
Application of selection logic to a novel pseudocode algorithm in the paired construction task (continued_learning segment).

raters.rationale: Construction-and-trace task exceeds pure recall; no strategic/extended reasoning across cases required, so capped at DOK 2.
Agrees DOK 2; construction plus trace-testing against provided data is application, not strategic thinking.

key_vocabulary.definition: A boolean expression evaluated to determine branch taken.
A path of execution following a decision point; everyday meaning relates to trees/organisations.

preparation.additional_adult_role: Circulate during paired construction, prompting pairs with a stalled decision diamond using a 'what happens if the condition is false?' hinge question.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Display a pseudocode IF/ELSE snippet from last lesson; ask pupils to trace it silently for two given inputs.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Trace pseudocode independently and write predicted output for each input.

check_for_understanding.question: For input mark = 45, which branch of the grading algorithm executes?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Proceed to introduction if >80% correct; otherwise re-trace as a class first.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Present the standard GCSE flowchart symbol set (process, decision, start/stop, connector) and model mapping the Do Now pseudocode onto a decision diamond.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Annotate their own symbol reference sheet alongside teacher's live model.

check_for_understanding.question: Which symbol represents a decision point?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Advance if near-unanimous; otherwise re-model with a second worked example.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Fully worked example: convert a second, slightly more complex pseudocode algorithm (nested condition removed for this stage) into a complete flowchart on the board, thinking aloud.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Watch and question; no independent construction yet.

check_for_understanding.question: Why does the False branch need its own arrow back to a shared end point?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Move to guided practice regardless, using response to calibrate scaffolding level.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Set paired flowchart construction task from the printed pseudocode grading algorithm; circulate using least-help-first prompting.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: In pairs, construct a flowchart, then trace it against two provided test inputs to check behavioural equivalence with the pseudocode.

check_for_understanding.question: Does your flowchart produce the same output as the pseudocode for mark = 39?
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Pairs who fail the trace revisit the provide_model example before resubmitting.

teaching_episode.teacher_actions: Cold-call two pairs to present their flowcharts under the visualiser; class checks for missing branches or symbol errors.
teaching_episode.pupil_actions: Compare own flowchart against presented examples; correct any discrepancies.

check_for_understanding.question: Exit ticket: draw the decision diamond and both branch labels for 'IF age >= 18 THEN adult ELSE minor'.
check_for_understanding.decision_rule: Notation mastery posterior for flowchart updated from exit ticket accuracy; below-threshold pupils flagged for retreat/reinforcement next lesson.
